content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against `master`, read the planned-elsewhere/final-review/milestone persistence code end-to-end, and ran `cargo test --quiet` successfully. I found three correctness issues: mapped-bead comments are never flushed after posting, verification results are not written back so mappings stay permanently unverified, and the journal-first write path has no recovery for a missing `planned_elsewhere.ndjson` sidecar.

## Amendments

1. After posting a planned-elsewhere `br comment`, run `br sync --flush-only` (or batch comments before a final sync) so the comment mutation is durable. Reconciliation syncs at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:200-209` and only then posts comments via `src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:285-313`; `comment_bead` marks the adapter dirty until `sync_flush` runs (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:510-512`, `src/adapters/br_process.rs:607-617`), so successful comments can be lost on exit.
   Rationale: Without a second sync, the new optional-comment path is not actually persisted, which breaks the acceptance criterion for durable mapped-bead comments.
2. Persist post-run verification results back into milestone state/journal. The engine records every mapping as `mapped_bead_verified: false` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7613-7618`), and reconciliation filters on that flag (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:699-718`), but `verify_and_comment_mappings` only returns outcomes and never updates `planned_elsewhere.ndjson` or the journal (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:247-323`). As written, a verified mapping stays permanently 'unverified', so any reconciliation replay will re-verify and re-comment the same bead.
   Rationale: This breaks idempotency and means verification state is not durable across restart even though the feature is supposed to survive controller replays.
3. Make the journal and `planned_elsewhere.ndjson` writes recoverable/atomic. `record_planned_elsewhere_mapping` writes the journal first and says the sidecar can be rebuilt from it (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2348-2390`), but `load_planned_elsewhere_mappings` only reads the sidecar (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2395-2401`) and the engine merely logs and continues on sidecar-write failure (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7621-7657`). A crash or I/O error after the journal append leaves a mapping present in audit history but invisible to later reload/comment processing (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:684-702`).
   Rationale: The current write ordering creates a real crash window where planned-elsewhere mappings stop being durable milestone state despite the code comments claiming they are recoverable from the journal.
